q1.title: Is LilSwap a separate lending protocol?
q1.desc: No. LilSwap is not a lending protocol and does not custody funds. Transactions interact directly with the official Aave contracts and your position remains in Aave at all times.

q2.title: Why are your fees lower than the official interface?
q2.desc: In DeFi, hidden service fees (like ~0.05% on debt swaps and adapter fees on collateral) are often treated as 'normal'. At LilSwap, we challenge this standard. Our architecture is designed to interact directly with the Aave protocol, eliminating intermediate execution fees on debt swaps (0.02% service fee) and reducing collateral swap costs by 67%. We believe savings should stay with the user, ensuring full transparency about the real cost of your operation.

q3.title: Do I need to migrate my positions to LilSwap?
q3.desc: Not at all. There is no 'migration' or deposit needed. Since LilSwap operates as a direct interface for the Aave V3 protocol, your existing positions will show up automatically the moment you connect your wallet. You can start optimizing your debt and collateral instantly without moving a single cent.

q4.title: Is LilSwap a general-purpose token aggregator?
q4.desc: No. While standard aggregators are designed for swapping any tokens in your wallet, LilSwap is a specialized tool built exclusively for Aave V3 positions. We focus solely on converting debt and collateral within the protocol, providing highly optimized execution paths that general-purpose aggregators simply aren't designed to handle.

q5.title: What are the service fees on LilSwap?
q5.desc: Our biggest savings are in collateral swaps, where we charge approximately 0.10%, with rates starting as low as 0.05% for correlated pairs, a significant reduction compared to the ~0.30% total cost on official interfaces. For debt swaps, we charge a minimal 0.02% service fee. These low rates are possible because our architecture interacts directly with Aave's smart contracts, eliminating expensive middleware, passing the maximum efficiency directly to you.
